meta.title: Lev TMux Control Plane
meta.description: Prior Art Inventory, Adoption Matrix & Feature Plan for the unified tmux graph harness

props.title: TMux Control Plane
props.subtitle: The best tmux graph-based harness on the planet — assembled, not built from scratch
props.meta: Prior Art Sweep · 2026-04-10 · 6 agents, 25+ artifacts, 3 CASS bands

props.content: 4,000+ LOC of tmux orchestration code already exists across 5 languages, 6 projects, and 3 layers (Python POC, TypeScript runtime, Rust intake). clawhip (MIT, 34K LOC Rust) is the vendor source. NTM provides the protocol contract. OMX provides the MCP patterns. This is a unification project with a massive head start.

props.label: LINEAGE
props.title: The Oh-My Family Tree
props.subtitle: Every project that contributed tmux orchestration patterns to Lev

props.title: ⚙️ clawhip
props.content:
**Rust · 34,040 LOC · MIT**
Event-to-channel notification router with production-grade tmux lifecycle. Session launch, watch, attach, monitor. Prompt delivery with TUI readiness detection, retry, content-hash verification. Circuit breaker, rate limiter, timer wheel, daemon mode, cron. Has `omc.rs` (Claude launcher) and `omx.rs` (Codex launcher). **PRIMARY VENDOR SOURCE.**

**Key modules:** tmux_wrapper.rs · source/tmux.rs · hooks/prompt_deliver.rs · dispatch.rs · core/circuit_breaker.rs · core/rate_limit.rs · core/timer_wheel.rs · lifecycle.rs · daemon.rs · cron.rs · monitor.rs · omc.rs · omx.rs

**Strip:** Discord/Slack sinks, render/ templates
**Keep:** Everything else — the tmux core IS the lev-tmux crate

props.title: 🎯 NTM (Named Tmux Manager)
props.content:
**Go · v1.7.0 · MIT+Rider**
Full tmux session orchestrator. spawn/send/attach/dashboard/palette. Robot mode (--robot-*) for structured JSON output. Work intelligence (wraps BV). Agent Mail, locks, checkpoints. Serve API on port 7337. Pipeline YAML workflows. **PROTOCOL + CONCEPT SOURCE.** Rider blocks OpenAI/Anthropic only — fine for Lev.

props.title: 🔮 OMX (oh-my-codex)
props.content:
**Node/Rust · v0.12.4 · MIT**
Codex-specific orchestration. $ralph (persistence loop), $team (tmux parallel workers with worktree isolation), $autopilot, $deep-interview. 5 MCP servers. HUD state, auto-nudge, tmux hooks, notify fallback. **MCP + OPERATOR PATTERN SOURCE.**

props.title: 🔵 OMC (oh-my-claude)
props.content:
**Rust (via clawhip omc.rs) · MIT**
Claude Code launcher integration. Auto-resolves workdir/session/project from git. Hook check before launch. Lifecycle event emission (agent.started/finished/failed). Prompt delivery via tmux send-keys with TUI init delay.

props.title: 🦞 OpenClaw (oh-my-openagent)
props.content:
**TypeScript/Bun · No license**
Zod schemas for TmuxLayoutSchema (5 layouts), TmuxIsolationSchema (inline/window/session). Confidence scoring for OpenCode prompts. Lazy tmux binary resolution. **SCHEMA CONCEPTS ONLY** (no license = skip code).

props.title: 🐍 clawteam
props.content:
**Python · 613 LOC · No license**
Multi-agent tmux backend. Spawns agents per team, handles workspace trust prompts (7 agent types). Prompt injection via load-buffer/paste-buffer. **CONCEPT ONLY** (no license = skip code).

props.title: 🧪 agentctl (POC)
props.content:
**Python · 720 LOC · Internal**
TmuxController + TmuxAgentBackend. Graph backend spawning AI agents in tmux windows. Per-provider completion/failure regex. Nudge patterns, monitoring snapshots. **ARCHIVED but validated the graph-backend concept.**

props.label: LATE DISCOVERIES
props.title: Additional Projects Worth Studying
props.subtitle: Found during deep workshop sweep — not in original family tree but architecturally relevant

props.title: 🔧 agent-orchestrator (@composio/ao)
props.content:
**TypeScript · 3,288 tests · npm published**
tmux IS the default runtime (`runtime: tmux` in config). Agent-agnostic fleet manager — spawns parallel tmux sessions per issue. Plugin architecture with 8 swappable slots (runtime, agent, workspace, tracker, SCM, notifier, terminal, lifecycle). Dashboard at localhost:3000. **Study the plugin slot architecture for lev-tmux.**

props.title: 🦷 openfang (Agent OS)
props.content:
**Rust · 137K LOC · 1,767 tests · MIT**
Full OS for autonomous agents — 14 crates, single ~32MB binary. Agent kernel architecture, A2A communication, OFP peer networking, cron, budget/cost tracking. Different scope but the **Rust crate architecture** is worth comparing for lev-tmux module boundaries.

props.title: 🐝 claude-swarm
props.content:
**Ruby · v0.3.2 · RubyGem**
Orchestrates multiple Claude Code instances via YAML topology and MCP inter-instance communication. Git worktree isolation per instance. Session monitoring (`ps/show/watch`). Mixed AI providers. **MCP-based coordination** is the alternative pattern to tmux-based.

props.label: DECISIONS
props.title: Adoption Matrix
props.subtitle: What to take from where — Adopt (vendor code), Wrap (use external), Concept (take pattern), Skip (no license or not useful)

props.label: HERMES + OPENCLAW
props.title: Operator Shell Adoptions
props.subtitle: Where external projects outperform Lev — ceremony, onboarding, gateway contracts

rows.feature: MCP client seam for external runtimes
Typed WS + HTTP on one gateway
Worker health / restart / unified team view

props.label: REJECT
props.title: Explicit Do-Not-Vendor
props.subtitle: Things that look useful but conflict with Lev's architecture — documented so we don't re-evaluate

props.title: Provider-owned local state as Lev truth
props.content: `.codex/`, `.omx/`, `~/.hermes/` as canonical state → conflicts with XDG discipline and fractal config. Lev state lives under `.lev/` and XDG paths only.

props.title: OpenClaw Guardian git rollback
props.content: `git reset --hard` as a repair mechanism → conflicts with Lev's evidence-first posture. Staged remediation concept is good; the implementation is rejected.

props.title: Copying orchestration ontology wholesale
props.content: From OMC, Hermes, or OpenClaw → Lev already has FlowMind + prompt-stack + harness. Take ceremony and edge patterns, not core orchestration.

props.title: Hermes MCP server as Lev primary runtime
props.content: Wrong abstraction layer. Hermes MCP client seam is useful (WRAP), but their MCP server as runtime is rejected — Lev has its own daemon/harness stack.

props.label: DOC LANDSCAPE
props.title: Current Document Sprawl
props.subtitle: Existing specs, designs, and DNA touching this feature space

rows.covers: DAG, queue, loop, entity pipeline. NTM pool DELETED.
Loop subsystem, idle detection, circuit breaker

props.label: TRIAGE
props.title: Doc Sprawl Action Plan
props.subtitle: 90 docs inventoried — here's what to do with each category

rows.effort: Small — add sections to existing docs

rows.docs: spec-lifecycle-hooks.md (phantom refs), 3 stale chat/voice planning artifacts
hooks proposal → new spec, NTM PRD → new spec, Wave 4 plan → reference
70+ validated/done plans, 6 approved specs, 7 DNA files, 9 designs

props.label: NEW DOCUMENTS
props.title: What Gets Written
props.subtitle: The doc sprawl consolidation — 1 new spec, 1 new DNA file, 3 updates

props.title: 📋 spec-tmux-control-plane.md
props.content: **NEW — THE spec.** Consolidates: coordination design, NTM PRD, hooks proposal, Wave 4 plan. Covers: Rust lev-tmux crate, session lifecycle, spawn/wait FlowMind actions, session_mode, NTM robot-protocol compat, workstream attachment, TUI rendering.

props.title: 🧬 dna/core/tmux-harness.dna.yaml
props.content: **NEW — Constraints for the Rust crate.** tmux_socket_isolation, prompt_delivery_verified, session_registry_arc, idle_detection_pane_active, circuit_breaker_on_failure, lifecycle_event_emission, content_hash_before_send.

props.title: ✏️ Update flowmind.dna.yaml
props.content: **ADD constraint:** session_mode_awareness — FlowMind must declare session_mode (attended|unattended|cron) and route behavior accordingly. Attended = human can interrupt. Unattended = full autonomy with handoff. Cron = fire-and-forget.

props.title: ✏️ Update orchestration.dna.yaml
props.content: **ADD constraint:** spawn_wait_contract — Orchestration providers must implement spawn(backend, count, config) → session_ids and wait(session, condition, timeout) → result. Backends resolve via poly registry.

props.title: ✏️ Update spec-workstream.md
props.content: **ADD section:** Session Attachment — Workstreams gain an `active_sessions` field listing tmux session IDs. Human attaches/detaches via `lev workstream attach <id>`. FlowMind reads attachment state to set session_mode.

props.label: THE FEATURE
props.title: lev-tmux: Rust TMux Control Plane
props.subtitle: Vendor clawhip (MIT), strip Discord, keep the tmux core, extend for Lev

props.content: OMX bundles 3 layers: prompt corpus, workflow skills, tmux/state/operator runtime. Lev already has the first two. The differentiated layer is the tmux/operator runtime. — Gateway Promotion Handoff, 2026-04-05

props.title: KEEP (the lev-tmux crate)
props.content:
• tmux_wrapper.rs — session lifecycle
• source/tmux.rs — registry, polling, content hash
• hooks/prompt_deliver.rs — TUI readiness + retry
• dispatch.rs + router.rs — event routing
• core/ — circuit breaker, rate limit, timer wheel
• omc.rs + omx.rs — agent launchers
• lifecycle.rs — install/update/uninstall
• daemon.rs — daemonization
• cron.rs — scheduling
• monitor.rs — session monitoring
• config.rs — TOML config

props.title: STRIP (Discord/Slack specifics)
props.content:
• discord.rs, slack.rs — notification sinks
• sink/discord.rs, sink/slack.rs — delivery adapters
• render/default.rs — Discord message formatting
• client.rs — Discord daemon client
• dynamic_tokens.rs — Discord token rotation
• keyword_window.rs — Discord keyword batching
• events.rs — Discord event types (IncomingEvent)

props.title: P0: Vendor + Strip
props.content:
**clawhip → lev-tmux Rust crate**

Copy clawhip src/ to community/lev-tmux/ (or core/tmux-harness/). Strip Discord/Slack sinks. Replace IncomingEvent with LevEvent. Wire to Lev daemon protocol. Add DNA constraints. Ship basic `lev tmux spawn/send/watch` CLI.

props.title: P1: FlowMind Integration
props.content:
**spawn/wait + session_mode**

Implement FlowMind spawn/wait action handlers routing to lev-tmux. Add session_mode to FlowMind schema. Workstream session attachment. NTM robot-protocol compatibility (lev-tmux speaks --robot-* JSON). Shadow worker (ollama classifier, lev-r4q13).

props.title: P2: TUI + Multi-Runner
props.content:
**tmux floating pane workstream dashboard**

Workstream navigator via tmux display-popup. Keyboard shortcuts for session switching. Manage N looping orchestrators (Claude/Codex/Gemini/Composer). Live HUD with token budgets, exit reasons, entity progress. All rendered in tmux panes — no Electron, no browser.

props.label: ARCHITECTURE
props.title: Unified Control Plane (Corrected)
props.subtitle: tmux + exec are ONE path, not two. Constraint engineering is the differentiator. 4 O's patterns are blended in.

props.content:
graph TD subgraph HUMAN["Human Layer"] TUI["tmux TUI
floating panes · keyboard nav · workstream dashboard"] Attach["attach / detach
session_mode: attended · unattended · cron"] end subgraph GATE["Entry Gate (from OOO + OMX)"] Socratic["Socratic Interview
ambiguity scoring ≤ 0.2
mathematical readiness gate"] end subgraph DNA["Constraint Engineering (Lev only)"] Precedence["Architecture > Core > Feature
conflicts surface as tradeoff artifacts
feature DNA proposes, system resolves"] Gates["DNA Gates + Ratchet (C4)
constraints never demote
ValidatorChain + drift scoring"] end subgraph FLOWMIND["FlowMind Control Plane"] Session["Session Engine (session.ts)
progressive disclosure · pause/resume"] Flows["tick-loop · heartbeat · dna-cascade
spawn/wait actions · session_mode routing"] end subgraph EXEC["Unified Execution (tmux + exec converged)"] Harness["lev-tmux Rust Harness
clawhip vendor + 4O patterns
spawn · send · capture · idle · circuit break"] Adapters["Adapter Registry
Claude · Codex · Gemini · OpenCode
NTM compat (--robot-* protocol)"] Loop["Heartbeat + Ralph Loop
entity pipeline · A2A dispatch
budget · until · circuit breaker"] end subgraph AGENTS["Agent Surface"] Workers["tmux sessions (10+)
each agent = pane with lifecycle events
idle detection · content hashing · auto-nudge"] end TUI --> Attach Attach --> Socratic Socratic --> Precedence Precedence --> Gates Gates --> Session Session --> Flows Flows --> Harness Harness --> Adapters Adapters --> Loop Loop --> Workers

props.title: What Each Layer Absorbs from the 4 O's
Entry Gate
props.content:
**From OOO:** Socratic interview, ambiguity scoring (≤ 0.2 threshold)
**From OMX:** $deep-interview skill, readiness checks
**Lev adds:** DNA constraint algebra (C1-C5), formal readiness not just scoring

props.title: Constraint Engineering
props.content:
**Lev only — no equivalent in 4 O's.**
Architecture > Core > Feature precedence. Gate ratchet (C4). Formal axioms. OOO has convergence metrics (≥ 0.95) and drift scoring — adopt those as measurement, keep our algebra as enforcement.

props.title: FlowMind Control Plane
props.content:
**From OOO:** Double Diamond topology as flow, stagnation/oscillation detection on loops
**From OMX:** $ralph persistence loop, $autopilot pipeline
**From OMO:** Category-based model routing (visual/deep/quick)
**Lev adds:** Multi-surface codegen, adversarial FlowMinds, fractal scope

props.title: Unified Execution (tmux + exec = ONE path)
props.content:
**From clawhip:** tmux lifecycle, prompt delivery, circuit breaker, daemon, cron
**From OMX:** Team workers with worktree isolation, HUD state, auto-nudge
**From OMC:** Claude-specific trust prompts, hook-envelope routing
**From NTM:** --robot-* protocol, work intelligence, mail+locks
**NOT separate branches.** The Rust harness IS the exec path for CLI agents.

props.title: 1. Write spec-tmux-control-plane.md
props.content: Consolidate coordination design + NTM PRD + hooks proposal + Wave 4 plan into one approved spec. DNA cascade can then generate constraints.

props.title: 2. Vendor clawhip → lev-tmux
props.content: Copy MIT-licensed Rust code. Strip Discord sinks. Wire LevEvent bus. Add Cargo workspace member. Run `cargo check`.

props.title: 3. DNA cascade on the spec
props.content: `lev exec --flow dna-cascade` produces tmux-harness.dna.yaml + updates to flowmind/orchestration DNA. Then manifest-code generates the FlowMind handlers.

props.title: 4. Fix Poly namespace routing
props.content: Unblock `lev workstream create` CLI path. Required for auto-scaffolding on session boot. Two-word command routing in discovery.ts.
